bbc.title: 'Fingers on the trigger': Deadly warnings for Iranians being urged to take action
bbc.description: Iran's police chief has warned that his forces would treat anyone taking to the streets "at the enemy's request" as an enemy.

bbc.title: China approves 'ethnic unity' law requiring minorities to learn Mandarin
bbc.description: The law states that children should be taught Mandarin before kindergarten, until the end of high school.

bbc.description: Modelling agent used businesses to recruit girls and arrange US visas to visit Jeffrey Epstein, Brazilian women tell BBC.

bbc.title: War in Ukraine spills into Hungarian election campaign
bbc.description: Hungary is going to the polls soon and Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has one public enemy in his sights - Ukraine's president.

bbc.title: Noma head chef resigns from restaurant amid abuse allegations
bbc.description: René Redzepi had previously apologised after ex-employees of the Danish restaurant accused him of creating a toxic work environment.

bbc.title: Hozier, Jessie Buckley and Bruce Springsteen record Shane MacGowan tribute album
bbc.description: The album will also feature Primal Scream, David Gray, Jessie Buckley, Johnny Depp and Kate Moss.

bbc.title: Death toll from Ethiopia floods and landslides rises to more than 60
bbc.description: Forecasters say storms are becoming more intense in the region, partly due to global warming.

bbc.title: The Pussycat Dolls confirm reunion: 'We're celebrating where we're at as women'
bbc.description: Nicole Scherzinger, Ashley Roberts and Kimberly Wyatt announce a new single and world tour.

wsj.title: Palestinians Stream Back to Northern Gaza on Foot
wsj.description: Israel allowed displaced Gazans to begin crossing a military zone that bisects the enclave after a deadlock over hostage releases was broken.

wsj.title: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes
wsj.description: Troubles at Vanke raise questions about the continued spread of the property crisis and whether the Chinese state will step in.

wsj.title: Freed Israeli Hostages Still Had Shrapnel in Their Bodies From Oct. 7 Attack
wsj.description: Some of the women were held alone for extended periods and spent eight months in tunnels, an Israeli medical official said.

wsj.title: Suspected Sabotage of Deep-Sea Cable Triggers First NATO-Led Response
wsj.description: The alliance mounted its first coordinated response to a suspected sabotage campaign against critical infrastructure after another cable was severed in the Baltic Sea.

wsj.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City
wsj.description: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

wsj.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
wsj.description: Attacks by powerful militias against civilians reflect the state’s inaction eight years after a peace accord removed a powerful rebel group from the field.

wsj.title: Italy Supports Saudi Arabia Joining Fighter-Jet Program, PM Meloni Says
wsj.description: The U.K., Italy and Japan have already partnered on the Global Combat Air Programme, which aims to put a new stealth fighter with supersonic capability in the skies by 2035.

wsj.title: Shippers Wary of Red Sea Routes Despite Houthi Pledge to End Targeting
wsj.description: The world’s top three container operators said they fear instability in Gaza and broader regional tensions mean continued danger.

wsj.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
wsj.description: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

wsj.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
wsj.description: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

wsj.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
wsj.description: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

wsj.title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
wsj.description: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on Colombia.

wsj.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
wsj.description: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

wsj.title: Natural Gas Falls on Shifting Weather Forecasts
wsj.description: U.S. natural gas futures fell after weekend updates took some of the chill out of early February weather forecasts.

wsj.description: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

bloomberg.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Weigh Energy Prices and Rate-Cut Bets
bloomberg.description: Gold traded in a narrow range, after the release of US inflation data dimmed prospects for interest-rate cuts and the war in the Middle East pushed oil prices higher.

bloomberg.title: Qatar Aluminum Plant to Avert Full Halt as Gas Is Secured

bloomberg.title: Stock Traders Brace for More Short-Term Jitters as War Rages On
bloomberg.description: Equity traders are hedging against the risk of weeks of further volatility before any sense of calm returns to global markets.

bloomberg.title: Global Bonds Erase 2026 Gains as War Fuels Inflation Angst
bloomberg.description: Global bonds have surrendered their year-to-date gains as elevated oil prices stoke fears that inflation will reignite, triggering a selloff across fixed-income markets.

bloomberg.title: Blue Owl Tells Investors Its Loan Sale Had No Hidden Incentives

bloomberg.title: Venezuela Needs Its Oil Expats to Come Home. Many Won’t Go
bloomberg.description: A global diaspora of oil workers holds the key to the country’s recovery, but many still don’t trust the government. Peter Millard explains.  (Source: Bloomberg)

bloomberg.description: India has asked China to allow the sale of some urea cargoes as the war in the Middle East curtails the nation’s gas supplies, threatening fertilizer production in the agricultural powerhouse.

bloomberg.title: Iran Steps Up Attacks on Dubai and Shipping as War Escalates
bloomberg.description: Iran escalated attacks on parts of Dubai and shipping assets, heightening concern about the length of the Middle East war and the impact on already volatile energy markets.

bloomberg.title: Putin Grabs Opportunity From Global Oil Chaos
bloomberg.description: A Kremlin envoy met Trump officials in Florida yesterday to discuss the energy crisis.

bloomberg.title: Russia to Slash Spending Even as Iran War Boosts Oil Prices
bloomberg.description: Russia plans to scale back this year’s budget spending to rein in a widening deficit and avoid an increase in borrowing, even as surging oil prices boost revenue.

bloomberg.title: War Is Pushing Iran’s Water Supply to the Brink of Collapse
